config._comment_strategy: Cypher strategy: the record value is bound to `event`. MERGE on the deterministic node_id guarantees idempotency -- reprocessing the same node event updates the existing node instead of creating a duplicate. coalesce() makes the query tolerant to BOTH the field names actually emitted by Task 2 (label/lineno/col_offset) and the Task 3 TOPIC_CONTRACT aliases (name/line_start/column_start). Nested `properties` is flattened to a JSON string because Neo4j cannot store a nested map as a property.
config.neo4j.cypher.topic.cpg.nodes: MERGE (n:CPGNode {node_id: event.node_id}) SET n.file_id = event.file_id, n.file_path = coalesce(event.file_path, event.path), n.node_type = event.node_type, n.label = coalesce(event.label, event.name), n.code = event.code, n.lineno = coalesce(event.lineno, event.line_start), n.col_offset = coalesce(event.col_offset, event.column_start), n.end_lineno = coalesce(event.end_lineno, event.line_end), n.end_col_offset = coalesce(event.end_col_offset, event.column_end), n.schema_version = toString(event.schema_version), n.event_time = event.event_time, n.properties_json = apoc.convert.toJson(event.properties)
